text.title: pm-framework-focus-areas Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for pm-framework-focus-areas. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Treat focus areas as aspects of ongoing work, not sequential phases; Planning and Monitoring and Controlling occur throughout the project lifecycle, not just once.
rule.rationale: Conflating focus areas with phases re-introduces waterfall thinking and misrepresents how agile and hybrid projects operate.

rule.statement: Do not apply all 40 processes by default; document which processes are adopted and which are tailored out, with justification.
rule.rationale: Applying the full menu creates overhead that exceeds value, especially for small or agile projects.

rule.statement: Closing is never optional; even purely agile projects require a final retrospective across all sprints and a lessons-learned record.
rule.rationale: Skipping Closing leaves no institutional memory and no formal signal that the project is done.

rule.statement: For agile projects, document the focus-area mapping in the charter (sprint planning = Planning + Executing slice; sprint review = Monitoring and Controlling) so reviewers do not get lost.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit mapping, reviewers apply predictive assumptions and flag missing artefacts that have agile equivalents.

rule.statement: Use "focus area" as the canonical term; never "process group" when working in PMBoK 8 context.
rule.rationale: Mixing PMBoK 6 process group names with PMBoK 8 focus area names causes vocabulary drift and audit confusion.

rule.statement: Abbreviate Monitoring and Controlling consistently as "MnC" within a project; pick one abbreviation and pin it.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent abbreviations (M&C, MnC, MC) create diffs that are hard to read and audit tools that fail silently.
